description: Tier 1 — deterministic intake-pipeline eval. Runs the real pipeline (runEmailIntake / runCallIntake) over labeled records and asserts the exact structured outputs the broker relies on: carrier + load resolution, confidence/human-verification, compliance, cross-reference validation, best offer, and the recommendation. No LLM, no API key — fully reproducible and safe to run in CI.

cases.description: Clean email: MC resolves the carrier, load id resolves exactly to an OPEN load, no compliance issues. Baseline happy path.
Carrier has CONDITIONAL authority + not onboarded. Even though the load is OPEN, the broker must NOT book. The recommendation must say so.
Carrier insurance expired before the dataset 'now'. Load is covered (not open). Compliance flag must surface and recommendation must note the load is not open.
Inbound references a cancelled load. The pipeline must not treat it as bookable and the recommendation must say it's cancelled.
Call with no load number: MC resolves the carrier and structured lane/equipment search confidently locks onto the open load (100%, no human verification needed).
Structured load match is weak (lane/equipment but rate/status off). Must flag needs_human_verification and the recommendation must ask to confirm before quoting.
Carrier reads a 9-digit (mistyped) load number. Fuzzy id recovery finds the intended load but flags it for human verification, and validation explains the near-miss.
Garbled/unknown MC: the carrier is resolved by fuzzy NAME instead, and validation must flag both the unknown MC and the name-only match.
Voicemail with no load: scoping falls back to the carrier and surfaces this carrier's best prior offer (cross-record best-rate retrieval).
